key.name: accounts
key.description: One JSON-encoded {"label", "path"} entry per account. An empty list means a single auto-detected account at ~/.claude/.credentials.json with no switcher. A blank path on an entry also falls back to that default location.

key.name: active-account-index
key.description: Index into the accounts list of the account currently shown.

key.name: panel-label-style
key.description: How the active account is labeled next to the panel icon in multi-account mode: the full label, just its first letter, or hidden. The menu switcher always shows full labels.

key.name: panel-metrics
key.summary: Limits shown in the panel tag
key.description: Limit keys (e.g. 'session', 'weekly_all', 'weekly:Fable') whose percentage appears in the panel tag. The special value '*' means every limit the account has. An empty list hides all numbers.

key.name: beacon-metrics
key.summary: Limits that drive the beacon color
key.description: Limit keys whose utilization can change the beacon state (icon shape and tint). The special value '*' means "follow panel-metrics" — the beacon reflects the worst limit shown in the tag, so the color is always explained by a visible number. An empty list leaves the beacon untinted.

key.name: metric-label-style
key.description: How tag numbers are written: plain ('10/50/82'), with a one-letter hint per limit ('s10 w50 F82'), or with full names ('Session 10% Weekly 50% Fable 82%', spaced apart).

key.name: known-limits
key.description: One JSON-encoded {"key", "name", "letter"} entry per limit the extension has ever seen in a usage response, across all accounts. Written by the extension; the preferences dialog reads it to offer per-limit toggles without doing its own fetching.

key.name: auto-renew-signin
key.description: When true, Pharos renews an expired access token itself with the stored refresh token (one POST to Anthropic's token endpoint, the same flow Claude Code uses) and saves the rotated tokens back to the credentials file so Claude Code stays in sync. When false, Pharos is strictly read-only: it never calls the token endpoint and never writes a byte to disk — when the token expires, usage pauses until Claude Code next renews it.

key.name: refresh-seconds
key.description: How often to poll the usage endpoint in the background so the panel color stays current even while the menu is closed. Set to 0 to disable background polling (usage is then fetched only when you open the menu).

key.name: caution-threshold
key.description: Utilization at or above this shows the caution beacon (light on the horizon, gold).

key.name: warn-threshold
key.description: Utilization at or above this shows the warning beacon (full beam, ember).

key.name: crit-threshold
key.description: Utilization at or above this shows the critical beacon (on the rocks, red).
